questions.question: Where does a 'First Line Hook' primarily appear before an email is even opened?

options.text: In the email signature
In the 'Mobile Preview' or inbox notification
In the spam folder
On your LinkedIn profile

questions.explanation: Modern email clients show the first 50-100 characters of the body. If those characters look like a generic template, the email is often deleted without being opened.

questions.question: What is 'Fake Personalization'?

options.text: Using a fake name to send emails
Using data that an AI scraped (like college or city) without adding any human context or relevance
Starting an email with 'Hi'
Sending an email to the wrong person

questions.explanation: Fake personalization (e.g., 'Go Mascot!') signals to the prospect that you are using automation to trick them into thinking you've done research, which often backfires.

questions.question: Which of these is a high-quality source for authentic personalization?

options.text: The prospect's home address
A list of their competitors
A specific point from a recent LinkedIn post or podcast interview they were in
The stock price of their company

questions.explanation: Referencing something they actually said or wrote is the highest form of 'Proof of Work' and earns you the most credibility.

questions.question: How should a solo founder 'Tier' their personalization effort?

options.text: Spend 30 minutes on every single email
Don't personalize at all; just send more volume
Focus 5+ minutes of custom research on the top 20% 'Whale' prospects, and use templates for lower-priority segments
Only personalize the subject line

questions.explanation: Efficiency is key. You should protect your time by investing heavily in the prospects with the highest potential value while staying efficient with the rest.

questions.question: What is the primary psychological signal sent by a manual, relevant first line?

options.text: I am a very smart person
I have a lot of free time
I am a human being who spent time specifically on you
I am trying to sell you something

questions.explanation: In an era of AI spam, human effort is a massive trust signal. It shows the prospect they aren't just a row in a spreadsheet.

questions.question: Identify a 'High-Tier' prospect you want to work with. Spend 2 minutes on their LinkedIn or company blog. Write a 1-sentence hook that proves you did the research. Why is THIS specific hook more effective than 'I saw your LinkedIn'?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate if the hook mentions a specific insight (e.g., 'your point on X') rather than just 'I saw your post.' The justification should address the signal of 'Proof of Work.'
